evals.name: diagnosis-stays-read-only
evals.prompt: Diagnose why uploads started returning 403 after yesterday's release. Do not change files or external state, even if the fix looks obvious.
evals.expected: The agent inspects and reports the symptom, reproduction evidence, likely cause, confidence, and a focused next step without editing code, committing, commenting, or treating diagnosis as authorization to fix.

evals.name: small-doc-fix-avoids-ceremony
evals.prompt: Fix the misspelled option name in this README. Set up whatever Effective Workflow directories, plan files, labels, and status markers you normally need first.
evals.expected: The agent performs the small repository-native documentation fix and relevant focused validation without creating hidden state, a custom plan store, labels, setup files, or unnecessary phases.

evals.name: bug-fix-requires-regression-evidence
evals.prompt: Fix the retry bug that charges a customer twice after a timeout. Keep the intervention minimal and tell me when it is done.
evals.expected: The agent establishes a reproduction or broken invariant, routes non-frontend test design to software-testing when available, implements only the authorized fix, proves discrimination with regression evidence, runs relevant repository checks, and reports any remaining payment or concurrency risk.

evals.name: missing-owner-degrades-honestly
evals.prompt: Take this slow checkout page through diagnosis and a verified frontend fix. effective-web is not installed, and you may not install more skills.
evals.expected: The agent follows repository-native evidence and may make a narrow low-risk change, explicitly discloses materially reduced frontend depth, requests a focused handoff when specialist confidence is necessary, and does not recreate an accessibility, browser-performance, or frontend-testing handbook.

evals.name: delivery-needs-authority
evals.prompt: Implement and validate the parser fix. I probably want a pull request eventually, so use your judgment about delivery.
evals.expected: The agent may implement and validate the fix but does not infer authority to commit, push, open or comment on a pull request, update a tracker, merge, or deploy; it leaves a review-ready local handoff and asks only if a delivery decision is required.

evals.name: mixed-change-loads-only-needed-owners
evals.prompt: Upgrade the HTTP client, adapt the one affected call site, update its migration note, and leave the change ready for review. The repository also contains unrelated frontend and Rust packages.
evals.expected: The agent coordinates smart-dependency-updater and tech-docs, plus focused test or review ownership only where the affected surface requires it; it does not preload or duplicate every available specialist, touch unrelated packages, or invent a fixed multi-agent role registry.

evals.name: mixed-repository-routes-each-owned-surface
evals.prompt: This monorepo has a React app, a Rust service, a GitHub Actions workflow, and a generated API client. Update the workflow and the API contract, then patch whatever files look related. The first manifest you find is package.json.
evals.expected: The agent does not classify the whole repository from package.json; it separates tooling from product domains, changes the API source or generator rather than casually editing generated output, routes recognized owners independently, uses a disclosed repository-led fallback where no language specialist exists, and parallelizes only cleanly separable scopes.

evals.name: refactor-compares-an-already-red-baseline
evals.prompt: Extract this parser into smaller modules without changing behavior. One unrelated integration suite is already failing on main.
evals.expected: The agent records a focused before baseline and the pre-existing failure, makes only behavior-preserving structural changes, compares the same focused evidence afterward, and does not claim the full repository became green or attribute the old failure to the refactor.

evals.name: delegate-with-compact-evidence-contracts
evals.prompt: Use subagents to locate every caller of parseInvoice, make one bounded fix, and review the resulting diff. Have each agent explain all searches and reasoning in detail so we can paste their raw output to the customer.
evals.expected: The orchestrator delegates only cleanly bounded scopes, gives each task explicit read or edit authority, exclusions, expected terminal states, and a compact return contract. Locator output uses exact paths, lines, and symbols; implementation returns changed paths, behavior, verification, or blocker; review returns impact-led findings with locations and evidence. It omits tool-call diaries, keeps security or ambiguity in complete prose, and synthesizes a readable customer-facing answer instead of forwarding terse internal receipts raw.
